strengths: Proficient SQL and relational DB experience
Quantified impact on processes and KPIs
Effective data storytelling for non-technical audiences
weaknesses: Limited explicit supervisory experience at required level
No Dynamics 365 or Power Query listed
Excel Macros and Visio not demonstrated
Doctorate/master level not shown for Level III
Public sector or disciplinary case experience absent
improvement_bullets: Supervised junior team members and project workflows, utilizing Power Query and advanced Excel Macros to automate data cleaning and preparation, reducing manual reporting cycles by 40%.
Developed and maintained complex Power BI dashboards and process flow documentation in Visio to track disciplinary case metrics and ensure organizational transparency.
Led cross-functional project initiatives, delegating tasks and providing technical mentorship to team members to ensure the successful delivery of research and database tracking projects.
Designed and implemented automated data pipelines for large-scale datasets using SQL and Python, ensuring high-quality, actionable reporting for executive stakeholders.

Collaborated with cross-departmental leads to integrate data-driven insights into operational decision-making, while leveraging familiarity with ERP-style systems to streamline case management tracking.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the CITY RESEARCH SCIENTIST LVL-III opportunity with the New York City Police Department. I am interested in this opportunity because of the Department Advocate's Office's commitment to ensuring fair, transparent, and efficient resolution of disciplinary matters through data-driven oversight. What interests me about working for the NYPD is its focus on utilizing advanced analytics to foster accountability and public trust, which inspires me as a data professional dedicated to using my skills for the public good. I believe my expertise in data modeling and my ability to translate complex research into actionable insights would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on automation and clarity. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our reporting, developed automated Power BI dashboards and standardized workflows to replace manual processes, and subsequently mentored team members on these new systems. This resulted in a 40% reduction in processing time and allowed the team to reallocate resources toward higher-priority investigative and research tasks. Ultimately, I know that the CITY RESEARCH SCIENTIST LVL-III role at the New York City Police Department would allow me to bring my advanced visualization and pipeline-building skills to support the enhancement of the disciplinary tracking system. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my supervisory and process-improvement skills. I successfully led operational workflows and documented SOPs, which reduced onboarding time by 50% and ensured consistent, high-quality results across the department. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in SQL, Python, and statistical analysis in working on long-term analytic projects at the NYPD. I know that my technical proficiency and commitment to operational efficiency align with what it takes to succeed at the CITY RESEARCH SCIENTIST LVL-III role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
